items.title: Dictionaries & Hash Maps
items.description: Python's dict is the hash table you'll actually use day-to-day; this topic explains the mechanism underneath.

items.title: Sets
items.description: Python's set is a hash table that stores only keys, with no associated values.

items.title: Linked Lists
items.description: OrderedDict combines a hash table with a doubly-linked list to preserve order and power LRU caches.

items.title: Big-O Complexity
items.description: Amortized analysis explains why dict operations are described as O(1) despite occasional O(n) rehashes.
